app.subtitle: Home Loan Simulation — Fixed & Floating
app.footer: All calculations are simulations. Actual figures may vary depending on bank policies.

nav.renameScenarioHint: Click pencil to rename this scenario

empty.title: KPR Simulation
empty.description: Fill in the form on the left — loan amount, tenor, and interest rate — to see the installment estimate and amortization table.
empty.calcError_desc: An error occurred while processing data. Try changing the interest rate configuration.

form.paymentMethodAnnuityHint: Fixed monthly payment; principal/interest ratio changes each month
form.paymentMethodFlatHint: Fixed principal; interest calculated from original balance (higher installments)
form.adminFeeHint: Admin fee paid once at disbursement
form.fixedOnlyDesc: Entire tenor uses a fixed interest rate
form.fixedSingleFloatDesc: Fixed period, then one variable rate
form.fixedTieredFloatDesc: Fixed period, then tiered variable rates
form.floatingRateFull: The entire tenor is covered by the fixed rate period. No variable period needed.
form.floatingTieredDesc: Set different rates for each period after the fixed period ends
form.earlyRepaymentSubtitle: Optional — simulate extra payments to pay off sooner
form.erDisclaimer: Extra payments reduce the principal balance and shorten the tenor. Monthly installments do not change unless the interest rate changes at a period boundary.
form.erErrStartMonth: Starting month must be a whole number ≥ 1
form.erErrEndMonth: Ending month must be a whole number ≥ 1
form.erErrLumpMonth: Month must be a whole number ≥ 1
form.feeInsuranceToggle: Include estimated life & fire insurance premiums
form.feeInsuranceDisclaimer: Estimated premiums paid upfront for the full tenor. Actual values are determined by the bank's insurance partner.
form.tierCoverageHint: Tiers must cover Month {{from}} (Yr {{yearFrom}}) to Month {{end}} (Yr {{yearEnd}}) with no gaps.
form.tooltipDownPayment: The minimum down payment in Indonesia is generally 15–20% of the property price per OJK regulations. A larger down payment means lower monthly installments and less total interest paid.
form.tooltipPaymentMethod: Annuity: fixed monthly payment throughout the loan, with the principal/interest ratio shifting each month. Flat: fixed principal each month, interest calculated on the original balance — higher early payments that decrease over time.
form.tooltipAdminFee: A one-time fee paid at loan disbursement. The amount varies by bank, typically Rp 500K – Rp 5M.
form.tooltipFixedRate: A fixed interest rate means your installment won't change during this period even if market rates rise. It gives certainty for financial planning.
form.tooltipFixedDuration: The fixed-rate period is usually 1–5 years. After it ends, the rate switches to the bank's floating (variable) rate.
form.tooltipFloatingRate: The variable rate follows a benchmark (usually BI Rate + spread) and can go up or down. It's typically higher than the fixed rate because it carries market risk.
form.tooltipEarlyRepayment: Extra payments reduce the principal faster, lowering total interest and potentially shortening your tenor. Check whether your bank charges an early repayment penalty first.
form.tooltipProvisionFee: A one-time bank processing fee, typically 1% of the loan amount, paid at disbursement.
form.tooltipBphtb: BPHTB (Property Acquisition Tax) is a purchase tax on property, generally 5% of the property value minus the tax-free threshold. Paid by the buyer.
form.tooltipInsurance: Life insurance covers the remaining loan if the borrower passes away. Fire insurance protects the property asset. Both are typically mandatory for KPR.
form.tooltipSyariahAkad: Murabahah: the bank buys the property and sells it to you at cost + agreed margin — fixed installments for the full tenor. MMQ (Musyarakah Mutanaqishah): shared ownership where the bank's share decreases over time as you pay — structured around ujrah (rental fee) instead of interest.
form.bankRatePickerNote: Reference rates as of {{date}}. Verify actual rates with your bank before applying.

results.seeResultsErrorsAria: Jump to the input form to fix errors
results.amortizationDesc: View monthly payment details throughout the loan term.
results.amortCaption: Monthly amortization schedule over {{count}} months: month, year, rate, installment, principal, interest, and remaining balance.
results.amortSubtitleVirtual: {{count}} months — scroll to see all. Rows optimized with virtualization.
results.installmentGroupsSubtitle: Installment changes when the interest rate changes
results.feesDisclaimer: Estimated costs. Actual amounts may vary depending on the bank, notary, and regional policies.
results.nextStepAffordDesc: See if the installment is safe relative to your income and monthly obligations.
results.nextStepRefiDesc: Use this scenario's results to check takeover or refinancing potential.

affordability.existingDebtHint: Personal loans, credit cards, vehicle installments, etc.
affordability.minSurplusHint: Minimum reserve you want to maintain
affordability.stressTestDesc: From first variable period · single rate for remaining tenor

refinancing.title: Refinancing Calculator
refinancing.promptFill: Fill in the remaining balance, current interest rate, and new offer to view the analysis.
refinancing.newRateHint: Rate offered by the target bank
refinancing.interestSavingsSub: Difference in total interest between options

scenarios.legendDesc: Color highlights are relative to the scenarios you entered — they do not represent an absolute best or worst value.
scenarios.legendBetter: Better — the most favorable value among the compared scenarios
scenarios.legendHigher: Higher — the largest value among the compared scenarios
scenarios.legendMidRange: Mid-range — falls between the other values
scenarios.legendFootnote: All figures are simulation estimates. Actual amounts may vary depending on each bank's policies and terms.

syariah.akadMurabahahDesc: Fixed margin — equal installments every month
syariah.akadMmqDesc: Declining ujrah — installments decrease over time
syariah.disclaimer: This simulation is an estimate only and does not replace an official offer, akad, or bank confirmation.
syariah.affordabilityMurabahahNote: Rate stress test not applied — Murabahah simulation uses a fixed margin.
syariah.refinancingNote: Refinancing simulation uses general assumptions. Actual Islamic financing values may differ by akad and bank.
syariah.pdfDisclaimer: This simulation is an estimate only. It does not replace an official offer, akad, or bank confirmation.

onboarding.step1Desc: Calculate mortgage installments with fixed, floating, or combined interest — including Islamic financing.
onboarding.step2Desc: Enter property price, down payment, tenor, and interest rates. Supports annuity and flat methods.
onboarding.step3Desc: Get an amortization table, installment chart, early repayment simulation, and a complete KPR fee breakdown.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.annuityA: Annuity is a payment scheme where the monthly installment stays constant throughout the loan. The interest component decreases each month while the principal portion increases — the total installment never changes.
faq.flatQ: What is the flat rate method?
faq.flatA: The flat method calculates interest on the original principal every month. Installments stay equal, but the effective rate is higher than the nominal rate. Commonly used for vehicle loans.
faq.fixedFloatingQ: What is the difference between fixed and floating rates?
faq.fixedFloatingA: Fixed: the rate stays constant for a set period (usually 1–5 years), giving installment certainty. Floating: the rate follows market benchmarks and can rise or fall once the fixed period ends.
faq.downPaymentQ: What is the minimum down payment for KPR in Indonesia?
faq.downPaymentA: Generally 15–20% of the property price for a first home, per OJK/BI regulations. Non-residential properties or second homes may require a higher down payment.
faq.bphtbA: BPHTB (Land and Building Acquisition Duty) is a tax paid by the buyer when acquiring property, generally 5% of the transaction value minus the non-taxable threshold (NPOPTKP).
faq.earlyRepaymentA: Extra payments beyond the regular installment directly reduce the outstanding principal, lowering total interest paid and potentially shortening the loan term — while the monthly installment stays the same.
faq.syariahQ: What is the difference between Murabahah and MMQ?
faq.syariahA: Murabahah: the bank buys the property then sells it at cost-plus-margin; installments are fixed for the full tenor. MMQ: joint ownership where the bank's share decreases monthly, so installments can decrease over time.
faq.accuracyA: Results are simulation estimates. Actual figures may differ depending on each bank's policies, notary fees, local taxes, and the interest rate at the time the loan agreement is signed.

ltv.title: Loan-to-Value (LTV)
ltv.disclaimer: Reference BI-style caps for landed houses (syariah +5%). Actual limits vary by bank and BI policy.

buyVsRent.title: Buy vs Rent
buyVsRent.intro: Compare building home equity by buying against renting while investing your cash.
buyVsRent.noBreakEven: Buying doesn't overtake within the horizon
buyVsRent.disclaimer: A simplified projection: the renter invests the down payment plus any monthly savings. Excludes maintenance, property tax, and sale costs. Assumptions are estimates.
buyVsRent.promptFill: Enter a monthly rent to compare buying vs renting.

flpp.title: FLPP Subsidized Mortgage
flpp.intro: Check if you qualify for FLPP (government-subsidized KPR) and see the installment at the subsidized 5% fixed rate.
flpp.disclaimer: FLPP rules and the income/price caps vary by region and year. These are reference defaults, not an official eligibility decision — confirm with a participating bank.
flpp.promptFill: Enter your monthly income to check FLPP eligibility.

maxProperty.title: How Much Home Can I Afford?
maxProperty.intro: Estimate the maximum property price from your income — before you have a specific home in mind.
maxProperty.maxDsrHint: Share of income for all debt; banks often cap at 30–40%
maxProperty.empty: Enter your monthly income to see how much home you can afford.
maxProperty.noteNoIncome: Enter your income to get an estimate.
maxProperty.noteDebtTooHigh: Your existing debt already uses up the DSR budget — no room for a new installment.
maxProperty.noteDownPaymentInvalid: Down payment must be between 0% and 99%.
maxProperty.noteExistingDebt: Existing debt lowers how much you can borrow.
maxProperty.noteSyariah: For Syariah financing, the rate is an estimated margin/ujrah, not bank-confirmed.
maxProperty.noteEstimateOnly: Initial estimate based on DSR and your rate/margin assumption.
maxProperty.disclaimer: This is an early estimate based on DSR and rate/margin assumptions. Confirm eligibility and the final offer with a bank.

decision.title: Decision Summary
decision.verdictSafe: Looking good — this loan is within your limits.
decision.verdictWatch: Doable, but tight — check the warnings below.
decision.verdictRisky: Over your limits — consider a larger down payment, smaller loan, or longer tenor.
decision.verdictIncomplete: Enter your income in Affordability for a full verdict.
decision.flagNegativeSurplus: Net surplus is negative at the highest installment
decision.flagRateShock: A +{{pct}}% rate rise already pushes DSR into risky
decision.flagLtvOver: Down payment is below the reference minimum for a first home
decision.flagInstallmentJump: Installment rises {{pct}}% when the variable rate kicks in
decision.bestScenario: {{label}} has the lowest total interest while staying affordable — worth comparing.
decision.disclaimer: For reference only. Not financial advice.
